channel.title: Whalesbook Financial News
channel.description: Latest Indian and global financial market news

item.title: NSE IPO Milestone: Regulator Panel OKs ₹1800 Cr Settlement
item.description: India's National Stock Exchange (NSE) is one step closer to its Initial Public Offering (IPO) following a recommendation by a Securities and Exchange Board of India (SEBI) appointed panel to settle pending legal disputes for approximately ₹1800 crore. This figure exceeds NSE's prior provision, highlighting the cost of long-standing governance and access allegations that have delayed its listing for nearly a decade. With regulatory clearance improving, NSE has appointed a record 20 banks for its IPO, signaling an accelerated path towards listing.

item.title: HCL Tech Sees Q4 Revenue Fall, Margins Shrink as Outlook Dims
item.description: HCL Technologies posted a disappointing Q4 FY26. Revenues fell 3.3% sequentially to ₹33,981 crore, reversing prior growth. Margins compressed 220 basis points to 19.5%, driven by higher employee costs. New deal wins (TCV) dropped to $1.93 billion from $3 billion, and AI revenue growth slowed. The company forecast muted FY27 services revenue growth of 1.5%-4.5%, lowering expectations and fueling valuation concerns.

item.title: Dolly Khanna Focuses on Industrial Turnarounds: 3 Old Economy Stocks Poised for Growth
item.description: Investor Dolly Khanna is pivoting towards "old-economy" industrial and manufacturing stocks, favoring companies with backward integration and niche growth. Prakash Industries, Chennai Petroleum Corporation, and Rain Industries exemplify this strategy, each featuring unique expansion plans and operational efficiencies despite their cyclical nature.

item.title: India Power Sector: Advait, Savita Oil, Quality Power Eye Growth Amid Premium Valuations
item.description: India's power equipment makers are set for a strong run, boosted by renewable energy goals and soaring demand from AI data centers. We look at three firms: Advait Energy Transitions, aiming high in green hydrogen with a costly valuation; Savita Oil Technologies, offering sustainable oils and cooling at a more reasonable price; and Quality Power Electrical Equipment, growing rapidly in high-voltage gear but also trading at a steep premium. The report details their strategies, growth drivers, and risks.

item.title: Private Credit Faces Rising Stress: Defaults Climb, Risks Grow?
item.description: The private credit market, a key part of the financial system since 2008, is facing new pressures. Higher interest rates are exposing weaknesses, leading to record defaults for smaller companies and funding issues like 'redemption gates' at some funds. While big institutions are confident, the market's lack of transparency and how risks were reshaped after 2008 create ongoing challenges.

item.title: Indian Railways: Record Freight Volume, Profit Growth Slows
item.description: Indian Railways is now the world's second-largest cargo carrier, hauling 1.6 billion metric tonnes in FY24-25 thanks to nearly complete Dedicated Freight Corridors. Yet, this volume surge shows slower growth in Net Tonne Kilometres (NTKM) and revenue that barely beats inflation. Electrification has saved ₹6,000 crore, but questions linger on underused diesel locomotives and the actual return on investment, especially as India relies heavily on coal for power.

item.title: RBI Tightens Forex Rules, Raising Volatility, Rupee Goal Fears
item.description: The Reserve Bank of India (RBI) has enacted new forex rules, banning banks from offshore NDF markets and capping onshore positions to support the rupee. While the measures show initial stability, critics argue they ignore offshore market dominance, disrupt arbitrage, and reduce regulatory predictability. These actions also conflict with India's aim to internationalize the rupee, possibly leading to greater inefficiency and volatility.
